abstract: Background: Histopathology Image Segmentation remains a critical challenge in managing Prostate Cancer. Here, we propose a novel approach leveraging Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) to address this gap. Methods: We evaluated our framework on a comprehensive cohort of clinical samples and patient profiles. Results: Our findings indicate that integrating Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) characterizes tumor heterogeneity in Prostate Cancer, achieving a sensitivity of 97.2% and reducing false positives by 30%. Conclusion: This study demonstrates that Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) represents a viable clinical diagnostic tool for Prostate Cancer management.

Methods: We evaluated our framework on a comprehensive cohort of clinical samples and patient profiles. Results: Our findings indicate that integrating Radiomics Feature Extraction characterizes tumor heterogeneity in Leukemia, reducing clinical turnaround times from 5 days to under 3 hours. Conclusion: This study demonstrates that Radiomics Feature Extraction represents a viable clinical diagnostic tool for Leukemia management.

abstract: Background: Clinical Trial Optimization remains a critical challenge in managing Prostate Cancer. Here, we propose a novel approach leveraging Deep Learning Models to address this gap.
